ddls:
CREATE TABLE "segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "start_value" REAL /* Start value of the segment range */,
  "end_value" REAL /* End value of the segment range */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the segment was last updated */,
  "uid" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the segment was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the segment */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the segment is active or archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was last updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the category */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the category is active or archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tag was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tag was last updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the tag */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the tag is active or archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segment_tags" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment */,
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id", "tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segment_categories" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id", "category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, analyst, researcher) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user account is active or deactivated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the segment */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the segment was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access event occurred */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segment_overlaps" (
  "overlap_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each overlap */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment */,
  "overlapping_segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the overlapping segment */,
  "overlap_type" TEXT /* Type of overlap (e.g., partial, full) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the overlap was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the overlap was last updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the overlap */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the overlap is active or archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("overlap_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_segment_overlaps_overlapping_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("overlapping_segment_id") REFERENCES segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_segment_overlaps_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nested_segments" (
  "nested_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each nested segment */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment */,
  "parent_segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent segment */,
  "nesting_type" TEXT /* Type of nesting (e.g., hierarchical, flat) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the nested segment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the nested segment was last updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the nested segment */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the nested segment is active or archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("nested_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_nested_segments_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_nested_segments_parent_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_segment_id") REFERENCES segments ("segment_id")
)
